title: Cloudflare Observability MCP query confirms outcomes-mcp worker received traffic
tool_used: mcp__MCP_DOCKER__query_worker_observability (Cloudflare Workers Observability MCP via Docker profile reinforcement_data_engineering)
interpretation: outcomes-mcp is actively receiving traffic on the deployed workers.dev URL. Observability returns five distinct trigger keys including the two paths the orchestrator exercises (/healthz and /mcp). POST /mcp count of 2 is consistent with the C4 valid-bearer probe (which returned 200 + initialize SSE) plus the tick-10 no-auth probe (which returned 401). The GET / and HEAD / traffic represents passive traffic and likely the operator's earlier browser checks plus background HEAD probes from the Cloudflare edge. The favicon hits come from browser sessions; the root banner returned successfully on each one.
evidence_window: 06:00:30Z through 06:29:30Z (30 minute relative offset; 59 series timestamps returned by observability)
next_action: C6 — put an AI Gateway in front of /mcp and verify gateway log entries; C5's observability surface remains the always-on diagnostic for live worker traffic.
